events.title: Neutrality Proclamation of 1793
events.summary: Washington declared U.S. neutrality in the war between Britain and France, establishing a precedent for avoiding entangling alliances and asserting executive authority over foreign policy.
sources: National Archives, Neutrality Proclamation records.
Library of Congress, Washington foreign correspondence.

events.title: Jay Treaty with Great Britain
events.summary: Washington supported the Jay Treaty to resolve outstanding issues with Britain, prevent war, and secure trade rights despite intense domestic opposition.
sources: Library of Congress, Jay Treaty papers.
National Archives, early diplomatic records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Washington established foundational principles of neutrality and diplomatic pragmatism in early U.S. foreign policy.
sources: Library of Congress, Washington presidential papers.
National Archives, early foreign policy archives.

events.title: Quasi‑War with France
events.summary: Adams oversaw an undeclared naval conflict with France, expanding the Navy and authorizing limited hostilities while avoiding full‑scale war.
sources: National Archives, Quasi‑War naval records.
Library of Congress, Adams diplomatic correspondence.

events.title: Convention of 1800
events.summary: Adams negotiated peace with France, ending the Quasi‑War and dissolving the 1778 alliance, reshaping U.S. diplomatic independence.
sources: Library of Congress, Convention of 1800 papers.
National Archives, early treaty records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Adams strengthened U.S. naval power and preserved peace through difficult diplomacy with France.
sources: Library of Congress, Adams presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Louisiana Purchase
events.summary: Jefferson acquired the Louisiana Territory from France, doubling U.S. territory and reshaping continental geopolitics.
sources: National Archives, Louisiana Purchase documents.
Library of Congress, Jefferson diplomatic papers.

events.title: Naval Action Against Barbary Pirates
events.summary: Jefferson deployed naval forces to end tribute payments to Barbary states, asserting U.S. maritime rights.
sources: Library of Congress, Barbary War correspondence.
National Archives, naval deployment records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Jefferson expanded U.S. territory and asserted naval power in the Mediterranean.
sources: Library of Congress, Jefferson presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: War of 1812
events.summary: Madison led the nation into war with Britain over maritime rights, impressment, and frontier conflicts, resulting in a reaffirmation of U.S. sovereignty.
sources: National Archives, War of 1812 records.
Library of Congress, Madison diplomatic correspondence.

events.title: Treaty of Ghent
events.summary: Madison’s administration negotiated the Treaty of Ghent, ending the war and restoring pre‑war boundaries.
sources: Library of Congress, Treaty of Ghent papers.
National Archives, diplomatic treaty records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Madison defended U.S. maritime rights and secured peace through the Treaty of Ghent.
sources: Library of Congress, Madison presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Monroe Doctrine
events.summary: Monroe declared the Western Hemisphere closed to European colonization, asserting a bold U.S. foreign policy stance against European intervention.
sources: National Archives, Monroe Doctrine documents.
Library of Congress, Monroe diplomatic papers.

events.title: Adams–Onís Treaty Implementation
events.summary: Monroe oversaw the acquisition of Florida from Spain and clarified U.S.–Spanish territorial boundaries.
sources: Library of Congress, Adams–Onís Treaty papers.
National Archives, territorial treaty records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Monroe established a defining U.S. hemispheric policy and expanded national territory.
sources: Library of Congress, Monroe presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Promotion of Pan‑American Diplomacy
events.summary: Adams advanced hemispheric cooperation through the proposed Pan‑American Congress, seeking to strengthen ties with Latin American republics.
sources: National Archives, Adams diplomatic initiatives.
Library of Congress, Pan‑American correspondence.

events.title: Defense of Neutral Trade Rights
events.summary: Adams maintained strong positions on neutral maritime rights amid global conflicts.
sources: Library of Congress, Adams maritime policy papers.
National Archives, early diplomatic records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Adams pursued principled diplomacy focused on hemispheric cooperation and maritime rights.
sources: Library of Congress, Adams presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Confrontation with France Over Indemnity Payments
events.summary: Jackson threatened retaliation against France when it delayed payment of agreed‑upon indemnities, prompting eventual compliance.
sources: National Archives, Jackson–France correspondence.
Library of Congress, indemnity dispute papers.

events.title: Recognition of Texas Independence
events.summary: Jackson formally recognized the Republic of Texas, shaping future U.S. expansion.
sources: Library of Congress, Jackson Texas recognition papers.
National Archives, diplomatic recognition records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Jackson used forceful diplomacy to secure U.S. claims and expand influence in North America.
sources: Library of Congress, Jackson presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Neutrality in the Canadian Rebellions
events.summary: Van Buren enforced neutrality during Canadian border uprisings, preventing escalation with Britain.
sources: National Archives, Van Buren neutrality records.
Library of Congress, Canadian rebellion correspondence.

events.title: Rejection of Texas Annexation
events.summary: Van Buren opposed immediate annexation of Texas to avoid war with Mexico.
sources: Library of Congress, Van Buren Texas papers.
National Archives, diplomatic policy records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Van Buren prioritized diplomatic caution to avoid war with Britain and Mexico.
sources: Library of Congress, Van Buren presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Signaled Support for Assertive Territorial Diplomacy
events.summary: Harrison’s brief presidency included early signals of support for westward expansion and firmer stances toward Britain, though death prevented implementation.
sources: National Archives, Harrison diplomatic drafts.
Library of Congress, early foreign policy correspondence.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Harrison’s foreign policy impact was minimal due to his short tenure.
sources: Library of Congress, Harrison presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy records.

events.title: Annexation of Texas
events.summary: Tyler pushed aggressively for Texas annexation, securing Senate approval through a joint resolution after treaty failure.
sources: National Archives, Texas annexation records.
Library of Congress, Tyler diplomatic papers.

events.title: Webster–Ashburton Treaty
events.summary: Tyler resolved U.S.–British border disputes in Maine and the Great Lakes region.
sources: Library of Congress, Webster–Ashburton papers.
National Archives, treaty negotiation records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Tyler reshaped North American borders and secured Texas annexation.
sources: Library of Congress, Tyler presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Mexican–American War
events.summary: Polk initiated and managed the war with Mexico, resulting in vast territorial gains for the United States.
sources: National Archives, Mexican–American War records.
Library of Congress, Polk diplomatic correspondence.

events.title: Oregon Boundary Settlement
events.summary: Polk negotiated the Oregon Treaty with Britain, establishing the 49th parallel as the boundary.
sources: Library of Congress, Oregon Treaty papers.
National Archives, boundary negotiation records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Polk expanded U.S. territory dramatically through war and negotiation.
sources: Library of Congress, Polk presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Firm Stance Against European Influence in the Americas
events.summary: Taylor signaled resistance to European interference in the Caribbean and Central America, reinforcing Monroe Doctrine principles.
sources: National Archives, Taylor diplomatic correspondence.
Library of Congress, Taylor foreign policy papers.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Taylor maintained a firm anti‑European posture consistent with Monroe Doctrine traditions.
sources: Library of Congress, Taylor presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Opening of Japan (Perry Expedition)
events.summary: Fillmore initiated the expedition that would open Japan to Western trade, sending Commodore Perry with presidential instructions.
sources: National Archives, Perry Expedition documents.
Library of Congress, Fillmore diplomatic papers.

events.title: Enforcement of Neutrality in Latin American Filibustering
events.summary: Fillmore cracked down on unauthorized U.S. expeditions attempting to seize territory in Latin America.
sources: Library of Congress, Fillmore neutrality correspondence.
National Archives, filibuster suppression records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Fillmore advanced U.S. influence in Asia while restraining rogue expansionism in Latin America.
sources: Library of Congress, Fillmore presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Gadsden Purchase
events.summary: Pierce acquired land from Mexico to facilitate a southern transcontinental railroad route.
sources: National Archives, Gadsden Purchase records.
Library of Congress, Pierce diplomatic papers.

events.title: Ostend Manifesto Controversy
events.summary: Pierce’s diplomats proposed acquiring Cuba from Spain by purchase or force, sparking domestic and international backlash.
sources: Library of Congress, Ostend Manifesto papers.
National Archives, diplomatic correspondence.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Pierce pursued aggressive expansionism that heightened sectional tensions and international scrutiny.
sources: Library of Congress, Pierce presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Utah Expedition (Foreign‑Domestic Hybrid)
events.summary: Buchanan sent federal troops to Utah Territory to confront the Mormon government, treating the conflict partly as a quasi‑foreign insurrection.
sources: National Archives, Utah Expedition records.
Library of Congress, Buchanan territorial correspondence.

events.title: Attempted Acquisition of Cuba
events.summary: Buchanan pursued diplomatic efforts to purchase Cuba from Spain, continuing expansionist ambitions.
sources: Library of Congress, Buchanan Cuba papers.
National Archives, diplomatic negotiation records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Buchanan’s foreign policy mixed expansionist aims with mismanaged territorial conflicts.
sources: Library of Congress, Buchanan presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Trent Affair Resolution
events.summary: Lincoln defused a major diplomatic crisis with Britain after the U.S. Navy seized Confederate envoys from a British ship, avoiding British intervention in the Civil War.
sources: National Archives, Trent Affair records.
Library of Congress, Lincoln diplomatic correspondence.

events.title: Prevention of European Recognition of the Confederacy
events.summary: Lincoln’s administration worked aggressively to prevent Britain and France from recognizing the Confederacy, using diplomacy and strategic communication.
sources: Library of Congress, Lincoln foreign policy papers.
National Archives, Civil War diplomatic records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Lincoln prevented foreign intervention in the Civil War and resolved the Trent crisis without war.
sources: Library of Congress, Lincoln presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Pressure on France to Withdraw from Mexico
events.summary: Johnson demanded French withdrawal from Mexico, supporting the collapse of Maximilian’s regime and reasserting Monroe Doctrine principles.
sources: National Archives, Johnson–France correspondence.
Library of Congress, Mexican intervention papers.

events.title: Purchase Negotiations for Alaska
events.summary: Johnson’s administration negotiated the Alaska Purchase with Russia, expanding U.S. territory.
sources: Library of Congress, Alaska Purchase papers.
National Archives, treaty negotiation records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Johnson reinforced hemispheric anti‑colonial policy and expanded U.S. territory through the Alaska Purchase.
sources: Library of Congress, Johnson presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Attempted Annexation of Santo Domingo
events.summary: Grant pursued annexation of Santo Domingo to expand U.S. influence in the Caribbean, though the Senate rejected the treaty.
sources: National Archives, Santo Domingo annexation records.
Library of Congress, Grant diplomatic papers.

events.title: Support for Arbitration in the Alabama Claims
events.summary: Grant resolved Civil War‑era disputes with Britain through international arbitration, securing compensation for damages caused by British‑built Confederate ships.
sources: Library of Congress, Alabama Claims papers.
National Archives, arbitration records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Grant pursued expansion in the Caribbean and advanced international arbitration as a diplomatic tool.
sources: Library of Congress, Grant presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Opposition to Filibustering in Latin America
events.summary: Hayes opposed private U.S. military expeditions in Latin America, reinforcing neutrality and hemispheric stability.
sources: National Archives, Hayes neutrality records.
Library of Congress, Hayes diplomatic correspondence.

events.title: Defense of U.S. Interests in the Pacific
events.summary: Hayes supported early U.S. involvement in Pacific trade routes and naval positioning.
sources: Library of Congress, Hayes Pacific policy papers.
National Archives, foreign policy records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Hayes reinforced anti‑filibustering policy and expanded U.S. strategic interest in the Pacific.
sources: Library of Congress, Hayes presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Support for Pan‑American Engagement
events.summary: Garfield planned a Pan‑American conference to strengthen hemispheric cooperation, though his assassination halted progress.
sources: National Archives, Garfield diplomatic drafts.
Library of Congress, Pan‑American correspondence.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Garfield’s foreign policy was limited but aimed at expanding hemispheric diplomacy.
sources: Library of Congress, Garfield presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy records.

events.title: Modernization of the U.S. Navy
events.summary: Arthur began the transition from wooden ships to a modern steel navy, strengthening U.S. global presence.
sources: National Archives, naval modernization records.
Library of Congress, Arthur defense correspondence.

events.title: Chinese Exclusion Enforcement in Foreign Context
events.summary: Arthur enforced the Chinese Exclusion Act while managing diplomatic tensions with China.
sources: Library of Congress, Arthur China policy papers.
National Archives, exclusion enforcement records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Arthur modernized U.S. naval power and navigated diplomatic fallout from exclusionary immigration policy.
sources: Library of Congress, Arthur presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Opposition to Hawaiian Annexation
events.summary: Cleveland opposed the annexation of Hawaii after a U.S.‑supported coup, calling for restoration of the Hawaiian monarchy.
sources: National Archives, Hawaii annexation records.
Library of Congress, Cleveland diplomatic papers.

events.title: Venezuelan Boundary Dispute
events.summary: Cleveland invoked the Monroe Doctrine in a boundary dispute between Venezuela and Britain, pushing for arbitration.
sources: Library of Congress, Venezuela boundary papers.
National Archives, diplomatic arbitration records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Cleveland defended hemispheric principles and resisted imperial expansion.
sources: Library of Congress, Cleveland presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Support for Hawaiian Annexation
events.summary: Harrison supported annexing Hawaii and submitted a treaty to the Senate, though it was not ratified before he left office.
sources: National Archives, Harrison annexation records.
Library of Congress, Harrison diplomatic papers.

events.title: Expansion of U.S. Naval Power
events.summary: Harrison accelerated naval modernization, laying groundwork for future global projection.
sources: Library of Congress, Harrison naval policy papers.
National Archives, defense modernization records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Harrison advanced expansionist policy and strengthened U.S. naval capabilities.
sources: Library of Congress, Harrison presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Cuban Independence Crisis
events.summary: Cleveland resisted calls for U.S. intervention in Cuba’s rebellion against Spain, maintaining diplomatic caution.
sources: National Archives, Cuba crisis records.
Library of Congress, Cleveland foreign policy papers.

events.title: Continued Opposition to Hawaiian Annexation
events.summary: Cleveland again blocked annexation efforts, citing improper U.S. involvement in the Hawaiian overthrow.
sources: Library of Congress, Cleveland Hawaii correspondence.
National Archives, annexation records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Cleveland maintained anti‑imperialist positions and resisted interventionist pressures.
sources: Library of Congress, Cleveland presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Spanish–American War
events.summary: McKinley led the U.S. into war with Spain, resulting in the acquisition of the Philippines, Guam, and Puerto Rico.
sources: National Archives, Spanish–American War records.
Library of Congress, McKinley diplomatic papers.

events.title: Annexation of Hawaii
events.summary: McKinley secured congressional approval to annex Hawaii, expanding U.S. presence in the Pacific.
sources: Library of Congress, Hawaii annexation papers.
National Archives, territorial acquisition records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: McKinley transformed the U.S. into an overseas imperial power through war and annexation.
sources: Library of Congress, McKinley presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Roosevelt Corollary to the Monroe Doctrine
events.summary: Roosevelt asserted the U.S. right to intervene in Latin America to stabilize nations and prevent European involvement.
sources: National Archives, Roosevelt Corollary records.
Library of Congress, TR foreign policy papers.

events.title: Panama Canal Acquisition
events.summary: Roosevelt supported Panama’s independence from Colombia and secured rights to build the Panama Canal.
sources: Library of Congress, Panama Canal correspondence.
National Archives, canal treaty records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Roosevelt expanded U.S. global influence through interventionism and strategic infrastructure.
sources: Library of Congress, Roosevelt presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Dollar Diplomacy
events.summary: Taft promoted U.S. financial investment abroad, especially in Latin America and East Asia, to expand influence without military force.
sources: National Archives, Taft diplomatic records.
Library of Congress, Dollar Diplomacy papers.

events.title: Intervention in Nicaragua
events.summary: Taft supported military and financial intervention in Nicaragua to protect U.S. interests.
sources: Library of Congress, Taft Nicaragua correspondence.
National Archives, intervention records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Taft emphasized financial leverage and selective intervention to expand U.S. influence.
sources: Library of Congress, Taft presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: World War I Entry
events.summary: Wilson led the U.S. into World War I to defend maritime rights and promote a new international order.
sources: National Archives, WWI records.
Library of Congress, Wilson wartime correspondence.

events.title: Fourteen Points and League of Nations Advocacy
events.summary: Wilson proposed a postwar framework for global peace and championed the League of Nations.
sources: Library of Congress, Fourteen Points papers.
National Archives, peace negotiation records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Wilson reshaped global diplomacy through wartime leadership and postwar internationalism.
sources: Library of Congress, Wilson presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Washington Naval Conference
events.summary: Harding convened a major international conference to limit naval armaments and reduce global tensions.
sources: National Archives, Naval Conference records.
Library of Congress, Harding diplomatic papers.

events.title: Return to Isolationist Tendencies
events.summary: Harding rejected U.S. membership in the League of Nations and emphasized limited international commitments.
sources: Library of Congress, Harding foreign policy correspondence.
National Archives, post‑WWI diplomatic records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Harding promoted arms limitation while steering the U.S. away from Wilsonian internationalism.
sources: Library of Congress, Harding presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Kellogg–Briand Pact
events.summary: Coolidge supported the Kellogg–Briand Pact, an international agreement renouncing war as national policy.
sources: National Archives, Kellogg–Briand records.
Library of Congress, Coolidge diplomatic papers.

events.title: Non‑Intervention in Latin American Conflicts
events.summary: Coolidge reduced military involvement in Latin America, favoring diplomatic solutions.
sources: Library of Congress, Coolidge Latin America papers.
National Archives, foreign policy records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Coolidge emphasized diplomatic agreements and reduced military intervention abroad.
sources: Library of Congress, Coolidge presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Stimson Doctrine
events.summary: Hoover’s administration declared that the United States would not recognize territorial acquisitions achieved by force, responding to Japan’s invasion of Manchuria.
sources: National Archives, Stimson Doctrine records.
Library of Congress, Hoover diplomatic papers.

events.title: Good Neighbor Policy Foundations
events.summary: Hoover reduced U.S. military involvement in Latin America, laying groundwork for the later Good Neighbor Policy.
sources: Library of Congress, Hoover Latin America correspondence.
National Archives, foreign policy records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Hoover advanced non-recognition principles and reduced military intervention in Latin America.
sources: Library of Congress, Hoover presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Good Neighbor Policy
events.summary: FDR renounced U.S. interventionism in Latin America, withdrawing troops and promoting diplomatic cooperation.
sources: National Archives, Good Neighbor Policy records.
Library of Congress, Roosevelt diplomatic papers.

events.title: World War II Alliance Leadership
events.summary: FDR forged the Allied coalition, coordinated global strategy, and shaped the postwar order through the United Nations.
sources: Library of Congress, WWII alliance correspondence.
National Archives, wartime diplomatic records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: FDR reshaped global diplomacy through wartime leadership and hemispheric cooperation.
sources: Library of Congress, Roosevelt presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Truman Doctrine
events.summary: Truman committed the U.S. to supporting nations resisting communist influence, beginning the policy of containment.
sources: National Archives, Truman Doctrine records.
Library of Congress, Truman Cold War papers.

events.title: Marshall Plan
events.summary: Truman implemented massive economic aid to rebuild Western Europe and prevent Soviet expansion.
sources: Library of Congress, Marshall Plan correspondence.
National Archives, European recovery records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Truman established the core architecture of U.S. Cold War foreign policy.
sources: Library of Congress, Truman presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Eisenhower Doctrine
events.summary: Eisenhower pledged U.S. support to Middle Eastern nations resisting communist influence.
sources: National Archives, Eisenhower Doctrine records.
Library of Congress, Middle East policy papers.

events.title: Covert Operations in Iran and Guatemala
events.summary: Eisenhower authorized CIA operations that overthrew governments in Iran (1953) and Guatemala (1954).
sources: Library of Congress, Eisenhower covert action papers.
National Archives, CIA operation records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Eisenhower expanded Cold War containment through alliances and covert interventions.
sources: Library of Congress, Eisenhower presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Cuban Missile Crisis
events.summary: Kennedy confronted Soviet nuclear missiles in Cuba, imposing a naval quarantine and negotiating withdrawal.
sources: National Archives, missile crisis records.
Library of Congress, Kennedy national security papers.

events.title: Alliance for Progress
events.summary: Kennedy launched a major aid program to strengthen economic development and democratic institutions in Latin America.
sources: Library of Congress, Alliance for Progress papers.
National Archives, foreign aid records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Kennedy managed the most dangerous nuclear confrontation in history and expanded hemispheric development initiatives.
sources: Library of Congress, Kennedy presidential papers.
National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Vietnam War Escalation
events.summary: Johnson dramatically expanded U.S. military involvement in Vietnam, committing hundreds of thousands of troops.
sources: National Archives, Vietnam escalation records.
Library of Congress, Johnson wartime correspondence.

events.title: Dominican Republic Intervention
events.summary: Johnson deployed U.S. forces to the Dominican Republic to prevent what he viewed as a communist takeover.
sources: Library of Congress, Dominican intervention papers.
National Archives, military deployment records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Johnson’s foreign policy was dominated by Vietnam escalation and Cold War interventionism.

National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Opening to China
events.summary: Nixon normalized relations with the People’s Republic of China, reshaping global geopolitics.
sources: National Archives, Nixon China visit records.
Library of Congress, Nixon diplomatic papers.

events.title: Détente with the Soviet Union
events.summary: Nixon negotiated arms control agreements including SALT I, easing Cold War tensions.
sources: Library of Congress, SALT I papers.
National Archives, Cold War diplomatic records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Nixon reoriented global strategy through China diplomacy and détente with the USSR.

National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Helsinki Accords
events.summary: Ford signed the Helsinki Accords, promoting human rights and stability in Europe.
sources: National Archives, Helsinki records.
Library of Congress, Ford diplomatic papers.

events.title: Final Withdrawal from Vietnam
events.summary: Ford oversaw the evacuation of U.S. personnel and allies during the fall of Saigon.

National Archives, crisis evacuation records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Ford advanced human rights diplomacy and managed the chaotic end of the Vietnam War.

National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Camp David Accords
events.summary: Carter brokered a historic peace agreement between Egypt and Israel.

Library of Congress, Carter Middle East papers.

events.title: Iran Hostage Crisis
events.summary: Carter faced a prolonged hostage crisis after militants seized the U.S. embassy in Tehran.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Carter achieved major diplomatic breakthroughs but struggled with the Iran hostage crisis.

National Archives, foreign policy archives.

events.title: Strategic Defense Initiative
events.summary: Reagan proposed a missile defense system to counter Soviet nuclear capabilities.

Library of Congress, Reagan national security papers.

events.title: Reykjavik Summit and Cold War Negotiations
events.summary: Reagan engaged in high‑stakes arms control negotiations with the Soviet Union, leading to the INF Treaty.

National Archives, arms control records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Reagan intensified Cold War pressure while advancing major arms control breakthroughs.

events.title: Gulf War Coalition Leadership
events.summary: Bush assembled a broad international coalition to expel Iraqi forces from Kuwait.
sources: National Archives, Gulf War records.

events.title: End of the Cold War Diplomacy
events.summary: Bush managed relations with a collapsing Soviet Union, supporting peaceful transitions in Eastern Europe.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Bush oversaw the peaceful end of the Cold War and led a major multinational military coalition.

events.title: NATO Intervention in the Balkans
events.summary: Clinton authorized NATO airstrikes in Bosnia and Kosovo to halt ethnic cleansing.

events.title: China Engagement and WTO Entry Support
events.summary: Clinton expanded economic and diplomatic engagement with China, supporting its entry into the WTO.

events.summary: Bush launched military operations in Afghanistan and Iraq following the 9/11 attacks.

events.title: Creation of New Security Alliances
events.summary: Bush expanded counterterrorism partnerships and reshaped global security frameworks.

National Archives, foreign policy records.

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Bush redefined U.S. foreign policy around counterterrorism and preemptive action.

events.title: Iran Nuclear Agreement
events.summary: Obama negotiated the JCPOA to limit Iran’s nuclear program in exchange for sanctions relief.

events.title: Pivot to Asia

events.title: Withdrawal from International Agreements
events.summary: Trump withdrew the U.S. from agreements including the Paris Climate Accord and the Iran nuclear deal.

events.title: Trade Confrontation with China

foreignPolicy.overallAssessment: Trump pursued unilateral foreign policy actions and confrontational trade strategies.

events.summary: Biden completed the withdrawal of U.S. forces from Afghanistan, ending America’s longest war amid chaotic evacuation scenes.

events.title: Reengagement with Allies and Multilateral Institutions
